name: kafkoroutine

metadata.summary: Asynchronous wrappers for kafka-python, integrating Kafka with Python's asyncio.

[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/mpol1t/kafkoroutine/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=RzC2e7FqAH)](https://codecov.io/gh/mpol1t/kafkoroutine)

# kafkoroutine

This repository offers Python-based asynchronous wrappers around `kafka-python`'s Producer and Consumer, bridging them
seamlessly with Python's `asyncio`.

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.7 or later
- A running Kafka instance (for actual message passing)


## Getting Started

Follow these instructions to integrate the asynchronous Kafka components in your asyncio-based Python project.

### Installation

Install `kafkoroutine` using pip:

```bash
pip install kafkoroutine
```

Please note: this project requires Python 3.7 or later and is built upon the `kafka-python` library.

## Usage

### AsyncKafkaConsumer

```python
from kafkoroutine.consumer import AsyncKafkaConsumer

async with AsyncKafkaConsumer(topics=topics, bootstrap_servers='localhost:9092', executor=None) as consumer:
    async for message in consumer:
        print(f"Received: {message.value.decode('utf-8')}")
```

### AsyncKafkaProducer

```python
from kafkoroutine.producer import AsyncKafkaProducer

async with AsyncKafkaProducer(bootstrap_servers='localhost:9092', executor=None) as producer:
    for msg in messages:
        await producer.send(topic, msg)
```

## Built With

* [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/docs/) - Packaging and dependency management
* [asyncio](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html) - Asynchronous I/O, event loop, and coroutines used for the
  implementation.
* [kafka-python](https://github.com/dpkp/kafka-python) - The Python client for Apache Kafka upon which these
  asynchronous wrappers are built.

## License

classes.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer

methods.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the AsyncKafkaConsumer.

```
param topics

The Kafka topic to consume from.

param bootstrap_servers

The Kafka server to connect to.

param executor

Executor to run blocking operations in.

param loop

Optional asyncio event loop.

param kwargs

Additional arguments to pass to the KafkaConsumer.
```

methods.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer.__aenter__
documentation: Enter the async context manager, creating the consumer if needed.

methods.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer.__aexit__
documentation: Exit the async context manager, closing the consumer.

methods.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer.create_consumer
documentation: Create the KafkaConsumer and its iterator in the executor.

methods.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer.__aiter__
documentation: Return self as the async iterator.

methods.name: kafkoroutine.consumer.AsyncKafkaConsumer.__anext__
documentation:
Get the next message from the KafkaConsumer.

If no more messages are available, raise StopAsyncIteration to end the iteration. If an exception occurs while
getting the next message, log the exception and raise it again.

An asynchronous Kafka Consumer wrapper.

This class is an asyncio-compatible version of the KafkaConsumer
from the kafka-python library.

methods.name: kafkoroutine.producer.AsyncKafkaProducer.send
documentation:
Send a message to the specified Kafka topic.

The actual sending is performed in the executor to not block the asyncio event loop.
\:param topic: The Kafka topic to send the message to.
\:param value: The message to send.
\:return: The result of the send operation.

An asynchronous Kafka Producer wrapper.

This class provides an asyncio-compatible version of the KafkaProducer
from the kafka-python library.
